news.summary: The Labrador Iron Ore Royalty Corporation's first quarter report for 2025 shows a decline in financial performance due to lower sales tonnages and iron ore prices. Royalty revenue was $35.6 million, 36% lower than 2024, with net income per share at $0.33, a 64% decrease. Sales tonnages were down 26%, with a drop in pellet and CFS sales significantly affecting revenue. IOC concentrate production decreased by 10% compared to 2024. Iron ore prices were down, with the 65% Fe index averaging $117/tonne. The outlook remains uncertain due to geopolitical developments. Royalty revenue was $35.6 million, with net income per share at $0.33. IOC's concentrate production decreased by 10% compared to 2024, and overall sales tonnages were down 26%. Iron ore prices were lower in the first quarter of 2025, with the 65% Fe index averaging $117 per tonne. The outlook for the second quarter is uncertain due to geopolitical developments. The Corporation had a positive net working capital of $22 million as of March 31, 2025.
news.headline: Labrador Iron Ore Royalty Corporation's first quarter report for the period ended March 31, 2025, showed a decrease in revenue to $36.2 million, net income at $21.4 million, and cash flow per share to $0.39 due to lower sales tonnages and iron ore prices. IOC sales tonnage dropped 26%, with CFS tonnages down 43% compared to the previous year. Iron ore price averaged US$117 per tonne in Q1 2025, 14% lower than Q1 2024. Cash flow was 37% lower than Q1 2024. The outlook for iron ore pricing in Q2 remained uncertain due to geopolitical developments involving US tariffs.

news.summary: A new report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in the past five years. This marks a significant improvement from the previous rate of 5.2%. The report also indicates that the economy added 300,000 new jobs last month, surpassing economists' expectations of 250,000. Additionally, the report highlights that the wage growth has increased by 3.8% in the same period. This data suggests a positive trend in the country's labor market and economic growth, providing hope for a continued recovery post-pandemic.

news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, on May 7, 2025, Enerflex Ltd. (TSX: EFX) (NYSE: EFXT) disclosed that its Annual Meeting of Shareholders took place online, where all 8 proposed directors listed in the Company’s Management Information Circular from March 21, 2025, were successfully elected. Shareholders voting on each individual nominee director during the Meeting constituted the basis for shareholder representation.
news.headline: Enerflex Ltd. shareholders approve all 8 nominee directors at Annual Meeting on May 7, 2025.

news.summary: In Kansas City, Mo., on May 7, 2025, NASB Financial, Inc. (OTCQX: NASB) released its financial report, revealing that for the quarter concluding on March 31, 2025, they recorded a net income of $5.8 million equivalent to $0.80 per share. This figure is notably lower than the numbers from the same quarter in the previous period, which stood at $8.0 million or $1.10 per share.
news.headline: NASB Financial, Inc. reported Q1 2025 net income of $5.8 million or $0.80 per share, down from $8.0 million or $1.10 per share.

news.summary: Rubellite Energy Corp. reported strong financial and operational results in the first quarter of 2025. They achieved record conventional heavy oil sales production of 8,339 bbl/d, exceeding guidance and representing an 8% increase from Q4 2024 and an 85% increase from Q1 2024. Total sales production reached 12,383 boe/d, up 19% from Q4 2024. Exploration and development capital expenditures totaled $22.3 million. Adjusted funds flow increased by 94% to $35.9 million, per share it was up by 30%. Net income in Q1 2025 was $1.2 million. Free funds flow reached $11.0 million. As of March 31, 2025, net debt stood at $147.7 million. The company had available liquidity of $33.1 million. Heavy oil sales volumes are expected to grow by 44-48% in 2025. Rubellite plans to spend $73 to $88 million for the rest of 2025 on exploration and development drilling programs. The article touches on their operations update for Greater Figure Lake, Frog Lake, Marten Hills, and other exploration activities. Rubellite will focus on reducing net debt and other balance sheet obligations with forecasted activity being funded from adjusted funds flow. The company is monitoring its capital spending plans due to the recent decline in oil prices. They plan to address end of life ARO with abandonment and reclamation expenditures of approximately $1.1 million for the remainder of 2025.
news.headline: Rubellite Energy Corp. reports Q1 2025 results: sales production up 8%, adjusted funds flow up 94%, net debt down to $147.7M.

news.summary: Calibre Mining Corp. has released their financial and operating results for the first quarter ended on March 31, 2025. The company, trading as TSX: CXB and OTCQX: CXBMF, shared updates on the Valentine Gold Mine situated in Newfoundland & Labrador, Canada. The consolidated Q1 2025 filings are accessible at www.sedarplus.ca and also on the company’s website at www.calibremining.com. It is important to note that all figures mentioned are in U.S. dollars unless specified otherwise.
news.headline: Calibre Mining reports Q1 results: Financial & production numbers; Valentine Gold Mine progress; merger with Equinox Gold set for Q2 2025.

news.summary: A recent report has revealed that there has been a significant increase in the number of confirmed COVID-19 cases in the United States. The total number of cases has reached over 10 million, with a record-breaking daily increase of 100,000 cases reported. Health officials are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing to help curb the spread of the virus. The alarming surge in cases has put a strain on hospitals, with over 50,000 COVID-19 patients currently hospitalized across the country. Authorities are considering implementing stricter measures to control the situation as the holiday season approaches.
news.headline: Concerns about Indian earnings decline subsides, lifting nascent stock rally; positive impact seen on markets.

news.summary: Energy Fuels has boosted its predicted uranium production for 2025 by 22% and its finished goods inventory guidance by a significant 193%. Additionally, the company has strengthened its working capital position. They are making progress in the production of high-grade U.S. uranium and world-class rare earth and mineral sand projects.
news.headline: Energy Fuels boosts 2025 uranium production by 22% and inventory by 193%, enhancing financial position and advancing key projects.

news.summary: Palo Alto, California - In a recent development, Institutional Property Advisors (IPA) has announced an expansion in Northern California. The move signifies IPA's increasing presence in the region. The specific details regarding this expansion, such as the exact locations involved, the number of properties included, and the financial figures associated with the expansion, have not been disclosed in the provided information. This expansion could potentially impact the real estate market in Northern California and bring new opportunities for investors and clients in the area.
news.headline: Institutional Property Advisors expands in Northern California, including Palo Alto, with a focus on growth.

news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia on May 7, 2025, Western Forest Products Inc. (TSX: WEF) disclosed the outcomes of its Annual General and Special Meeting of Shareholders. The meeting, which took place on the mentioned date, reported on the voting results.
news.headline: Western Forest Products Inc. (TSX: WEF) reports Annual General and Special Meeting voting results in Vancouver.

news.summary: MCAN Mortgage Corporation, also known as MCAN Financial Group, reported a net income of $16.6 million for the first quarter of 2025, down from $23.2 million in the same period in 2024. The decrease was mainly due to higher provisions for credit losses influenced by economic forecasts and geopolitical factors. Despite this, the company remains optimistic due to its strong liquidity, credit quality, and growth strategies. The return on average shareholders' equity for Q1 2025 was 10.99%, compared to 17.09% in Q1 2024. Total assets reached $5.4 billion, with non-securitized assets increasing to $3.0 billion, uninsured residential mortgages totaling $1.1 billion, and construction portfolio at $1.1 billion. Securitized mortgages decreased to $2.4 billion. In financial updates, net non-securitized mortgage spread income decreased by $1.7 million, while net securitized mortgage spread income increased by $0.6 million. There was a provision for credit losses on non-securitized mortgages of $3.1 million in Q1 2025 due to economic concerns impacting performing loans. The company aims to capitalize on growth opportunities amidst economic challenges, focusing on value creation for stakeholders.
news.headline: MCAN Financial Group's Q1 2025 net income was $16.6 million, down from $23.2 million in Q1 2024. They declared a 41 cents cash dividend. Total assets reached $5.4 billion, and uninsured residential mortgage originations increased to $97 million in Q1 2025. Non-securitized assets rose by 5.1% to $3.0 billion. Net non-securitized mortgage spread income dropped by $1.7 million. Net securitized mortgage spread income increased by $0.6 million. Provision for credit losses was $3.1 million in Q1 2025. CEO Derek Sutherland stated they are managing controllable factors to protect their bottom line and capitalize on opportunities in uncertain economic conditions.

news.summary: In Chicago on May 7, 2025, a record-setting rate hike request of $308.6 million by Nicor Gas is under scrutiny by advocacy groups. The Citizens Utility Board (CUB), Illinois PIRG, and Environmental Defense have submitted testimony to state regulators, stating that the proposed increase is excessive. They recommend reducing the rate hike by over 50%, highlighting concerns about the impact on consumers.
news.headline: Consumer advocates seek to reduce Nicor Gas' $308.6M rate hike by more than half, citing excessive profits and wasteful bonuses.

news.summary: The latest drilling results have revealed the highest grade yet with 60.8g/t AuEq over 0.4m, comprising of 51.3g/t Au, 7.2% Cu, and 18.0g/t Ag. The mineralization remains open, indicating a potential for further exploration. Additionally, there are assays pending from more drill holes, raising anticipation for potentially more positive results.
news.headline: Highly promising Golden Eye prospect with significant gold and copper, highest grade result of 60.8g/t AuEq, 51.3g/t Au, 7.2% Cu, 18.0g/t Ag; more results pending.

news.summary: A coalition of Democrat supporters of cryptocurrency is on the verge of endorsing the GENIUS Act again. The proposed bill for regulating stablecoins might go for a vote within the next 24 hours.
news.headline: Senate close to agreement on stablecoin bill as pro-crypto Democrats rally support for the GENIUS Act. Vote possible tomorrow.

news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, on May 7, 2025, Athabasca Oil Corporation (TSX: ATH) announced its first quarter results showing successful operational performance resulting in a significant return of capital. The company completed its second annual share buyback program, indicating a strong financial position. With low corporate break-evens, enduring assets, and a solid balance sheet, Athabasca is well-equipped to navigate through market fluctuations.
news.headline: Athabasca Oil reports 63% growth in funds flow per share, completes second annual share buyback program, shows strong financial position amid market volatility.
